items.title: Vector Policy Optimization: Training for Diversity Improves Test-Time Search
items.authors: Ryan Bahlous-Boldi, Isha Puri, Idan Shenfeld et al.
items.abstract: Language models must now generalize out of the box to novel environments and work inside inference-scaling search procedures, such as AlphaEvolve, that select rollouts with a variety of task-specific reward functions. Unfortunately, the standard paradigm of LLM post-training optimizes a pre-specifie...

items.title: The Matching Principle: A Geometric Theory of Loss Functions for Nuisance-Robust Representation Learning
items.abstract: Robustness, domain adaptation, photometric and occlusion invariance, compositional generalisation, temporal robustness, alignment safety, and classical anisotropic regularisation are usually treated as separate problems with separate method families. This paper argues that much of their shared struc...

items.title: Tokenisation via Convex Relaxations
items.authors: Jan Tempus, Philip Whittington, Craig W. Schmidt et al.
items.abstract: Tokenisation is an integral part of the current NLP pipeline. Current tokenisation algorithms such as BPE and Unigram are greedy algorithms -- they make locally optimal decisions without considering the resulting vocabulary as a whole. We instead formulate tokeniser construction as a linear program ...

items.title: Which Way Did It Move? Diagnosing and Overcoming Directional Motion Blindness in Video-LLMs
items.authors: Jongseo Lee, Hyuntak Lee, Sunghun Kim et al.
items.abstract: Video Large Language Models (Video-LLMs) have made rapid progress on temporal video understanding, yet many fail at a basic perceptual primitive: signed image-plane motion direction. On simple videos of a single object moving left, right, up, or down, most Video-LLMs perform near chance, with above-...

items.title: Cambrian-P: Pose-Grounded Video Understanding
items.authors: Jihan Yang, Zifan Zhao, Xichen Pan et al.
items.abstract: Camera pose matters. The position and orientation of each viewpoint define a shared spatial coordinate frame that relates observations across video frames. Yet this signal is largely absent from multimodal LLMs (MLLMs) for video understanding, which process frames as isolated 2D snapshots, instead o...

items.title: Integrable Elasticity via Neural Demand Potentials
items.abstract: We propose the Integrable Context-Dependent Demand Network (ICDN), a demand-first neural model for multiproduct retail demand. The model learns log-demand as a smooth, context-conditioned function of log-prices, allowing elasticities to be derived exactly from the learned demand surface. On the Domi...

items.title: AwareVLN: Reasoning with Self-awareness for Vision-Language Navigation
items.authors: Wenxuan Guo, Xiuwei Xu, Yichen Liu et al.
items.abstract: Vision-and-Language Navigation (VLN) requires an agent to ground language instructions to its own movement within a visual environment. While state-of-the-art methods leverage the reasoning capabilities of Vision-Language Models (VLMs) for end-to-end action prediction, they often lack an explicit an...

items.title: GesVLA: Gesture-Aware Vision-Language-Action Model Embedded Representations
items.authors: Wenxuan Guo, Ziyuan Li, Meng Zhang et al.
items.abstract: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have shown strong potential for general-purpose robot manipulation by unifying perception and action. However, existing VLA systems primarily rely on textual instructions and struggle to resolve spatial ambiguity in complex scenes with multiple similar objects. To...
